evidence: 274 @Test methods across 36 test files covering api, exploration, query, config, indexer, utils, and viz packages
StubQueryExecutor (shared base stub), TestExchangeFactory.MockExchange, and GrammarConfigHelper are dedicated test-infrastructure classes used consistently across tests — no ad-hoc duplication
BlackLabQueryExecutorTest and BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest both carry @Disabled on their live-index nested class/test; the real Lucene query path is never exercised in CI

BlackLabIntegrationTest is a CI-compatible integration test that exercises XML parsing → collocate extraction → logDice ranking using a StubCollocateHelper subclass — a well-designed substitute, but it bypasses BlackLabQueryExecutor entirely

WordSketchApiServerTest starts a real HTTP server on port 18080 and verifies route routing, but always uses the empty StubQueryExecutor, so no non-trivial data flow through the full pipeline is ever verified
ExplorationHandlersTest and SketchHandlersTest cover both 200 happy-path and 400 error-path for all major endpoints
SemanticFieldExplorerInternalTest.RecordingExecutor captures CQL pattern strings and asserts that they contain 'NN' — minor coupling to internal pattern construction details
No Mockito or mock-framework usage anywhere; hand-rolled stubs are decoupled from internal implementation
No snapshot tests, no timing-dependent assertions, no test-ordering annotations — no fragility patterns

strengths: Shared test infrastructure (StubQueryExecutor, TestExchangeFactory, GrammarConfigHelper) is factored into dedicated classes and reused consistently across all test packages, keeping setup concise
API handler tests cover both happy-path (200) and all major validation failure (400) cases for every handler class
BlackLabIntegrationTest exercises the full collocate pipeline (XML snippet parsing → frequency accumulation → logDice ranking) in a CI-safe way without a live index
No mock-framework coupling; hand-rolled stubs expose only the interface surface and are stable across refactors
No snapshot tests, no timing dependencies, no test-ordering annotations — the suite has no fragility patterns
test_strategy.issue_character: The gap is concentrated at one critical layer: the real BlackLabQueryExecutor (Lucene index queries) is permanently @Disabled in CI, so the actual data-retrieval path is never verified outside of a manually configured environment.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The test suite is well-organised, uses sound patterns, and achieves good coverage of business logic at the exploration, API, and utility layers — this pushes the score above 60. However, the permanently disabled live-index tests mean the most critical production path (Lucene corpus queries) has zero CI coverage, and the live-server test (WordSketchApiServerTest) only exercises the routing layer with an empty stub, providing no cross-module verification of real data flow. By the rubric, 'critical paths with zero test coverage' is a primary indicator; one such path here is the entire data-retrieval layer, which is a subsystem-level gap that keeps the score from reaching solid territory (80+). A score of 68 reflects a strong unit-testing culture with one significant structural hole.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor live-index tests are permanently @Disabled, leaving the core Lucene query path untested in CI
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutorTest has a nested @Disabled @DisplayName("LiveIndex") class containing all real-query tests; only the static BCQL-string-building helpers run in CI
BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest is a top-level @Disabled class with Assumptions.assumeTrue guards — never runs in standard CI
BlackLabIntegrationTest is a useful CI-safe substitute but exercises CollocateQueryHelper directly, bypassing BlackLabQueryExecutor and the Lucene open/search lifecycle
issues.suggestion: Create a lightweight in-memory Lucene index fixture (indexed in @BeforeAll from a small embedded CoNLL-U corpus) to exercise BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern and executeCollocations without external infrastructure. Remove @Disabled once the fixture is in place; keep Assumptions.assumeTrue only for the real on-disk index path.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServerTest exercises routing with an empty stub executor — no cross-module integration test verifies real data flows from HTTP request to JSON response
evidence: STUB_EXECUTOR is a bare StubQueryExecutor returning empty lists/zero for all operations
All assertions verify only HTTP status codes and top-level JSON keys (status, seed, edges) — no assertion ever checks that non-trivial collocate data is correctly serialised
No test in the suite wires a non-empty StubQueryExecutor through the full HTTP → handler → exploration → response chain to verify semantic correctness
issues.suggestion: Add a parametrised integration test that injects a StubQueryExecutor with canned collocate data (e.g., empirical/scientific for seed=theory) and asserts the response body contains the expected collocates, logDice scores, and edge weights — verifying the full serialisation path without a live index.

root_causes: The live-index dependency for BlackLabQueryExecutor was treated as an all-or-nothing constraint; no embedded-index fixture was introduced to bridge the gap between fully-stubbed unit tests and the disabled real-index tests
API server tests were written to verify routing/wiring concerns and never extended to cover data-flow correctness, leaving a gap between handler-level unit tests and end-to-end verification
